name: Mushroom Pate#3
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Rinse and pat dry mushrooms, then finely chop (about 5 cups); set aside.
3. In a large saucepan, melt butter. Add onion and celery; sauté until tender, about 5 minutes; set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat eggs and cream cheese until smooth. Stir in bread crumbs, salt, basil, rosemary, oregano, black pepper, reserved onion and celery mixture, and chopped mushrooms.
5. Butter a 7 x 4 x 2 inch loaf pan. Place wax paper on the long side and bottom of the pan, leaving a 1-1/2 inch overhang.
6. Pour mushroom mixture evenly into the pan. Cover with foil.
7. Bake until firm, about 1-1/2 hours.
8. Let cool in the pan until lukewarm. Remove from pan using the wax paper overhang; discard the paper.
9. Serve at room temperature with crackers as an appetizer, garnished with mushroom slices, grated carrot, and chopped onion if desired. Yield: 12 to 14 portions.
